title: bahelia longifolia
intro: Bahelia longifolia, also known as the Bael tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, sweet-scented fruit and medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiarrheal effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Bahelia longifolia include the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever and sore throat. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal remedies, such as teas, decoctions, and ointments, which are typically made by infusing the leaves, bark, or roots in hot water or oil.
